title: From The Temporary To The Eternal Lessons From The Life Of The Apostle Paul
scriptureForWeek: This week will cover several sections of scripture to highlight the importance of lifting our vision and gaining an eternal perspective.

days.title: Numbering Our Days
days.scripture: PSALM 90 Lord, you have been our dwelling place throughout all generations. Before the mountains were born or you brought forth the whole world, from everlasting to everlasting you are God. You turn people back to dust, saying, “Return to dust, you mortals.” A thousand years in your sight are like a day that has just gone by, or like a watch in the night. Yet you sweep people away in the sleep of death— they are like the new grass of the morning: In the morning it springs up new, but by evening it is dry and withered. We are consumed by your anger and terrified by your indignation. You have set our iniquities before you, our secret sins in the light of your presence. All our days pass away under your wrath; we finish our years with a moan. Our days may come to seventy years, or eighty, if our strength endures; yet the best of them are but trouble and sorrow, for they quickly pass, and we fly away. If only we knew the power of your anger! Your wrath is as great as the fear that is your due. Teach us to number our days, that we may gain a heart of wisdom. Relent, LORD! How long will it be? Have compassion on your servants. Satisfy us in the morning with your unfailing love, that we may sing for joy and be glad all our days. Make us glad for as many days as you have afflicted us, for as many years as we have seen trouble. May your deeds be shown to your servants, your splendor to their children. May the favor of the Lord our God rest on us; establish the work of our hands for us— yes, establish the work of our hands.
days.reflection: Do you know how many days you are going to live? According to one study done across 9 nations, the average person lives for about 25,915 days or around 71 years. How does that number hit you? Maybe it seems high, like you have forever, or maybe it seems surprisingly low, like, ” Wow, that’s it?” Every man is only given a set amount of time here on this earth. That makes what you do with your life really important. And as you have probably learned, you are not guaranteed all of those days. Lots of guys your age die in accidents, get sick, or face other tragedies. Your life matters. Your ordinary everyday life is a gift. You need to use it wisely and live with a sense of the shortness of life and the length of eternity. Moses (remember him, self to others?) wrote today's Psalm. It contains some of the most memorable words in the whole bible. Teach us to number our days, that we may gain a heart of wisdom. Moses wanted the children of Israel to live with wisdom. He didn’t want them getting sucked into the thinking and habits of the godless nations around them. He wanted them to remember the gift of life and not waste their newfound freedom on stupid things. He wanted wise hearts in a foolish world. People with foolish hearts live like they have all the time in the world. People with foolish hearts think they can put off important things until tomorrow. People with foolish hearts act like the people around them who don’t know God. People with foolish hearts never think about God, heaven, hell, or the deep questions of life. You don’t want to grow into a foolish man. But people with hearts of wisdom realize how valuable time is. People with hearts of wisdom seize the moment in front of them. People with hearts of wisdom think deeply about the important stuff in life that really matters. Questions like “How did we get here?” “Why are we here?” “Who should we trust?” “How should we live?” “Whose opinion really matters?” It can be really hard to focus on the big questions in life at your age. There are so many things that seem more important, more urgent, or more relevant to someone your age. But don’t believe it. Lift your eyes; think deep and wide. Number your days. You have one less today than you did yesterday; one you will never get again. Count them, and make them count. This is what wise men do.
questions: Do a quick Google search. How many days have you been alive so far? How many days does it predict you have left? How does that make you feel?
Think back over the last few weeks. What was one of your favorite days? Why?

Ask the person you are doing this study with to share one of their favorite days from the last few weeks. What made it good? Ask them to share how they learned to value time and think deeply about the real issues of life.

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. "LORD, remind me how brief my time on earth will be. Remind me that my days are numbered—how fleeting my life is. You have made my life no longer than the width of my hand. My entire lifetime is just a moment to you; at best, each of us is but a breath.” We are merely moving shadows, and all our busy rushing ends in nothing. We heap up wealth, not knowing who will spend it. And so, Lord, where do I put my hope? My only hope is in you. Psalms 39:4-7 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to change your perspective on time. Ask him for vision to see what really matters.

days.title: Careful Or Careless
days.scripture: EPHESIANS 5:15-17 Be very careful, then, how you live —not as unwise but as wise, making the most of every opportunity, because the days are evil. Therefore do not be foolish, but understand what the Lord’s will is.
days.reflection: Careful or careless? Which one better describes you? One of the best ways to waste your life is to be careless. Careless with what you watch. Careless with how you use your time. Careless with how you spend your money. Careless with your choices. Careless with your faith. Careless people end up making foolish mistakes that cause them tons of pain and problems. Their lives get riddled with shame and regret. You do not want to be a careless man. Wise men give careful thought to their ways. They are intentional about who they make friends with, careful about how they use their time, and careful about what they watch and where they go. Wise people understand that the best way to live is to find out what God wants and do that. They know that’s where the joy is, the fun lies, and the rewards are. That’s why Proverbs 28:26 says, “Those who trust in themselves are fools, but those who walk in wisdom are kept safe.” Wise hearts lead us to safety. The Apostle Paul, who is going to be your mentor for this shift, wrote these words with a sober heart. Paul wasted a huge chunk of his life. In his earlier years, he hated Jesus and worked really hard to destroy the church. He even worked to get Christians put in prison and approved of some of them getting put to death. But then he met Jesus and everything changed. He realized how foolish he had been. He realized that joy and life and freedom were found in a relationship with Jesus. So Paul didn’t want to waste any more time living as a fool. And he wanted the church at Ephesus (who he wrote this to) to use their time well too. He wanted to help them avoid the regrets he had and make the most of their lives. Paul knew that how we use our time was one of the keys to success in life. It’s the key to walking with God, the key to encountering joy, the key to avoiding spiritual danger. I think if you got to sit down and meet with Paul, he would say these same words to you. He would try and press upon your heart the urgency and importance of living from an eternal perspective, avoiding the drama and evil of this age, and lifting your vision to the things of God. You live in a pretty godless age. There is a lot of evil stuff happening all around you. Whether it is social violence, bullying, ungodly sexuality, or mocking religion and God, it can be easy to make foolish little compromises that end up tripping you up. Be on your guard, be wise, think big picture, and look beyond the moment. Live from a bigger story and bigger picture, one that will make you a rare and wise man for a guy your age.
questions: What are a couple of foolish things you see guys your age doing? What in our culture today seems to encourage this?
What are two or three things you see from this verse that can help you be wiser and care about your time?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share a few things that happened at your age that helped them be more careful about their life.

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. Do not be wise in your own eyes; fear the LORD and shun evil. This will bring health to your body and nourishment to your bones. Proverbs 3:7-8 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to show you any areas that you may need to pay attention to in your life and have more wisdom.

days.title: To Live Is Christ
days.scripture: PHILIPPIANS 1:20-26 I eagerly expect and hope that I will in no way be ashamed, but will have sufficient courage so that now as always Christ will be exalted in my body, whether by life or by death. For to me, to live is Christ and to die is gain. If I am to go on living in the body, this will mean fruitful labor for me. Yet what shall I choose? I do not know! I am torn between the two: I desire to depart and be with Christ, which is better by far; but it is more necessary for you that I remain in the body. Convinced of this, I know that I will remain, and I will continue with all of you for your progress and joy in the faith, so that through my being with you again your boasting in Christ Jesus will abound on account of me.
days.reflection: Do you like movies with plot twists? Some of the most iconic movies in history make you think one thing is happening for most of the movie and then “BAM” they flip everything around and it’s the opposite of what you thought. The guilty person is innocent. The innocent person is guilty. The person who is the enemy is actually a friend. The poor person is a billionaire in disguise. You know how it goes. Life is full of plot twists. What we are trained to think by the culture today and even some churches may actually be wrong. Here is a common way that life is presented in many churches: Live a great life, have lots of fun, get tons of money, a happy family, and some really cool toys. Then when you are dead, you get to go to heaven and be with Jesus. That idea goes something like this. To live is gain, to die Christ. Get all you can, have as much fun as you can, and then when you die, Jesus will help you avoid hell. This life is about you, and heaven is about Jesus. So do everything you want here because in heaven you are going to have to do what God wants. But Paul teaches us something amazing today. He flips that popular paradigm on its head. Paul said this in today's reading: To live is Christ and to die is gain Paul had a radical encounter with the person of Jesus and it changed his life. Jesus met him and saved him from his sins. He gave him a purpose to live for and a mission to complete. Paul was even taken up into heaven at one point (imagine that) and what he saw was so beyond words he longed to be there and be with Jesus for all eternity. So for Paul, he called people to a different version. Paul wanted people to leverage their lives for Jesus and not be distracted by the temporary stuff that doesn’t matter. He knew life in all its fullness was about walking with Jesus and then longing for heaven. Paul wanted to leverage his one and only life for the kingdom, and then be with Jesus. It just got better and better for Paul. Have you ever had a hobby or stuff you were into when you were younger but now look back on as kind of childish? Have you ever thought that you may think about the stuff you love now like that when you are older? Have you considered that you may think about most of the stuff in your life like that when you measure it against eternity? You were born to know Jesus, and it only gets better from there. You get to live in a new heaven and earth after your life of meaning and purpose is done. That’s a plot twist most people never see coming. Live into that story well.
questions: What is one of your favorite movies with a plot twist in it?
Can you describe the difference between Paul’s vision of life and death and the popular version often preached in churches today?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share how they flipped the paradigm and discovered the divine plot twist of life. How old were they, and what changed when it happened?

thoughts.verse: I will not set before my eyes anything that is worthless. I hate the work of those who fall away; it shall not cling to me.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to lift your vision from the distractions of the age to a vision of the kingdom of God.

days.title: Stamp Eternity On My Eyeballs
days.scripture: PHILIPPIANS 3:18-20 For I have told you often before, and I say it again with tears in my eyes, that there are many whose conduct shows they are really enemies of the cross of Christ. They are headed for destruction. Their god is their appetite, they brag about shameful things, and they think only about this life here on earth. But we are citizens of heaven, where the Lord Jesus Christ lives. And we are eagerly waiting for him to return as our Savior.
days.reflection: Do you know what a filter is? A filter is something you place over your eyes that changes everything you see. When you put sunglasses on, you are putting a filter over the light to block out the harsh rays. People put filters on photos when they post them on social media to make them look better than they are or to highlight certain colors. Sometimes hunters and soldiers put night vision goggles on so they can see in the dark. They are putting a filter over their eyes to see in the darkness. Lots of people only have one filter that they look through life with. The filter of the “here and now.” They think about the current moment and their own short lives as the ultimate reality. Our secular culture wants to put filters over your eyes that make you forget about anything more than your own passions and pleasure. It wants to block out God, his word, his values, and his kingdom. It wants to filter out Christian faith from the world we live in. Paul notices this and it made him weep. He said, Their god is their appetite, they brag about shameful things, and they think only about this life here on earth. It’s a tragedy when a man puts the wrong filter over his eyes and he misses out on the amazing things in front of him. Choose your filter carefully. And it's even more urgent than that. Satan blinds people so they don’t have a filter for the gospel. Look at what 2 Corinthians 4:4 says. “Satan, who is the god of this world, has blinded the minds of those who don’t believe. They are unable to see the glorious light of the Good News. They don’t understand this message about the glory of Christ, who is the exact likeness of God.” There was a famous preacher from a couple of hundred years ago named Jonathan Edwards. He looked around him and realized there had to be something better than just having fun and enjoying yourself. There had to be something more satisfying and meaningful. He became a follower of Jesus, and it changed his life. He wrote a little phrase that has been talked about hundreds of times since. This is a paraphrase of it. “Stamp eternity on my eyeballs.” What he was asking was to have a filter that let him see an eternal perspective on everything. A filter that made him see what God wanted, not just what the world wanted. Boys only have a filter for the temporary, but men have a different filter. They see everything through the lens of the eternal. Rick Warren says, “When you live in the light of eternity, your values change.” What filter is over your eyes? It’s never too late to change filters and stamp eternity on your eyeballs too!
questions: Read this quote by John Wesley: “I judge all things only by the price they shall gain in eternity.” What on earth do you think he means by that? How would it impact how a guy like you makes decisions?
In what ways do you see the “secular” filter over the movies and shows that our culture produces? Have you felt the culture trying to “filter out” God in your life?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share how they try and have an eternal perspective on things.

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. Turn my eyes from worthless things, and give me life through your word. Reassure me of your promise, made to those who fear you. Help me abandon my shameful ways; for your regulations are good. I long to obey your commandments! Renew my life with your goodness. Psalms 119:37-40 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to put a filter of eternal values over your eyes.

days.title: You Are Going To Die
days.scripture: ROMANS 6:19-23 Because of the weakness of your human nature, I am using the illustration of slavery to help you understand all this. Previously, you let yourselves be slaves to impurity and lawlessness, which led ever deeper into sin. Now you must give yourselves to be slaves to righteous living so that you will become holy. When you were slaves to sin, you were free from the obligation to do right. And what was the result? You are now ashamed of the things you used to do, things that end in eternal doom. But now you are free from the power of sin and have become slaves of God. Now you do those things that lead to holiness and result in eternal life. For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life through Christ Jesus our Lord.
days.reflection: Have you ever seen someone die? Not in the movies, but in real life? Has someone you care about passed away? Have you had a pet you cared about die? Death can be really, really heavy. Death is one of the greatest mysteries of life. So much of the music, art, and history of the world has been based around people wrestling with death. You too are going to die, and there is nothing you can do to stop it. So it would be really, really wise to prepare for your death, even now, because you don’t know when it will happen. You may not think this is that important because you are so young, but you need to prepare for eternity. John Owen, a theologian from a while back said this, "Satan's greatest success is in making people think they have plenty of time before they die to consider their eternal welfare." Have you really considered where you are going to spend eternity? It’s the most important thing you can do in your life. You may have been around church and faith and lots of talk about God, but have you personally resolved in your heart the place you want to go? Hebrews 9:27-28 makes this really, really urgent. It says, And just as each person is destined to die once and after that comes judgment, so also Christ was offered once for all time as a sacrifice to take away the sins of many people. He will come again, not to deal with our sins, but to bring salvation to all who are eagerly waiting for him. Death and judgment, eternal life and salvation, this is the choice before you. C.S. Lewis (he keeps showing up, and will for the rest of the Primal Path ☺ ) said this. Christianity asserts that every individual human being is going to live for ever, and this must be either true or false. Now there are a good many things which would not be worth bothering about if I were going to live only seventy years, but which I had better bother about very seriously if I am going to live for ever. The Bible says that the wages of sin are death. That’s why we die. But it also says that the gift of God is eternal life. Jesus came and died to take away our sins, so that they could not hold us in the grave any longer. On the cross, Jesus paid the penalty for our sins, and now we can live forever with him. This isn’t some pie-in-the-sky story but the central fact of history. Every Easter, Christians celebrate the resurrection of Jesus from the dead. Human history is dated by the death and resurrection of Jesus. In fact, your birthday is dated by the life of Jesus. You may not think about it often, but Jesus’ life changed the entire human calendar. This means the story is bigger than we realize. The story is bigger than our own lives. It means you have to choose to live into the greater story Jesus is telling rather than shrinking back into the smaller one just about you. Jesus said that he came to give us life, life more abundantly. Why don’t you choose to accept that life for yourself? It's better than you can comprehend.
questions: Have you ever really thought about the fact that you are going to die? Are you resolved and settled about what will happen when you do die?

Why do you think people never really think about their own death? Is there a healthy way to think about death? An unhealthy way? How do you think you can get that balance? (You may want to revisit the idea of “memento mori” here.)
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share how they chose to respond to Jesus’ offer of eternal life. When did it become real to them, and how did they say yes to it?

thoughts.verse: And this is the way to have eternal life—to know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ, the one you sent to earth.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to thank God for his offer for the forgiveness of your sins and his gift of enteral life. If you haven’t asked him to forgive you and opened your heart to a relationship with him, you can do that now.

extras: WEEK 1 MANSCHOOL THE DOT AND THE LINE MAN SCHOOL REMINDER The recipe for fun is pretty simple when you’re raising boys: add to any activity an element of danger, stir in a little exploration, add a dash of destruction, and you’ve got yourself a winner. - John Eldredge OPTION 1. ACTIVITY: THE DOT AND THE LINE It's hard to get a sense of the bigness of life, so sometimes it needs to be acted out. The dot and the line is an embodied take on the classic illustration about time and eternity. Watch this, and then go recreate this in a park: https://youtu.be/86dsfBbZfWs Buy a huge length of rope and make one little section red. Then talk about how long eternity is and how short the red part is. You may want to actually lay the rope out and walk the length of the whole thing. Then hold the tiny little part that is his life. This is something that will go the distance. Also, this is cool and mind-blowing. Watch this: https://youtu.be/nOVvEbH2GC0 Another option is to do the cemetery “What’s your dash” moment with your son, who may be a bit more open to the idea after this week. OPTION 2. WATCH IN TIME This is a fascinating movie that shows the way time affects human life.
FIELD NOTES PRAYER FOR MY SON
